description: The application team has filed 5 performance tickets for slow-running queries. These queries are documented in the HR.TUNING_QUERIES table (query_id, description, sql_text, performance_issue). A large test table HR.PERFORMANCE_ORDERS exists with 11,449 rows to simulate production load. Using Oracle SQL Developer: (1) retrieve and run each of the 5 queries from HR.TUNING_QUERIES through SQL Developer's Explain Plan feature to analyze their execution plans; (2) identify the performance bottleneck in each (look for TABLE ACCESS FULL operations on HR.PERFORMANCE_ORDERS); (3) create appropriate indexes on HR.PERFORMANCE_ORDERS to eliminate the full table scans — at minimum create indexes on the ORDER_AMOUNT, ORDER_DATE, and CUSTOMER_ID columns; (4) export a tuning analysis report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/tuning_report.txt documenting which queries were analyzed, what execution plan issues were found, and what indexes were created. HR credentials: hr/hr123. System credentials if needed: system/OraclePassword123.
